item.title: EU reaches deal to fine online platforms importing products deemed unsafe
item.description: The European Union has reached an informal agreement to fine вҖӢonline platforms importing unsafe products, in вҖҢa move which it said was aimed at tackling cheap and possibly unsafe Chinese goods вҖӢentering the EU via platforms вҖӢsuch as Temu and Shein.

item.title: New Mexico lays out options to force changes at Meta after court win
item.description: Talk about regulating social media to make it safer for children often gravitates around the U.S. Capitol and the EU headquarters in Brussels. But following a New Mexico jury verdict, a district court in Santa Fe carries some heft, too.

item.title: Stressed US grid forcing data centers to get more flexible
item.description: The U.S. technology industry is being pushed to shrink its power use in times of high demand, amid growing public concern вҖӢthat Big Tech's massive electricity needs for its expansion of data centers are maxing out the country's grid.

item.title: Exclusive: Constellation exec says grid operator told company Three Mile Island can't connect until 2031
item.description: U.S. grid operator PJM вҖӢhas told Constellation Energy that the former Three Mile вҖҢIsland nuclear power plant in Pennsylvania likely will not be able to connect to the grid until 2031, four years later than planned, the company said вҖӢat the CERAWeek energy conference in Houston on Thursday.

item.title: Big Oil to reap billions from Iran war windfall after a month of soaring energy prices
item.description: As Big Oil executives gathered this week and discussed the biggest-ever disruption to global energy supplies due to the war in Iran, there was one impact they did not address publicly: the multibillion-dollar windfall they will make because of soaring prices for the energy they sell.

item.title: Meta boosts Texas AI data center investment to $10 billion
item.description: Meta said on Thursday it is increasing вҖӢinvestment in its El Paso, Texas AI data center вҖҢto $10 billion, a more than sixfold jump, as it aims to hit 1-gigawatt capacity ahead of the facility's projected opening in 2028.

item.title: French education ministry reports TikTok to Paris prosecutor
item.description: Social media app TikTok has вҖӢbeen reported to the вҖҢParis prosecutor's department by the French education вҖӢministry over its вҖӢpossible links to mental health вҒ problems for young вҖӢpeople due to excessive вҖӢtime spent in front of screens, the education ministry вҖӢsaid on Thursday.

item.title: Food safety nonprofit's lawsuit claims Panera Bread misled consumers, citing Reuters
item.description: A food safety and animal welfare nonprofit filed suit against Panera Bread last week вҖӢbased on Reuters reporting, claiming the company misled вҖҢconsumers about PaneraвҖҷs meat products.

item.title: Exclusive: Occidental's Hollub, US oil's most powerful woman, prepares to hand over reins
item.description: Vicki Hollub's exit would mark an end to more than four decades at the Houston-based oil producer, where she became the first woman to become the CEO of a major oil company.

item.title: Mastercard explores sale of payments unit it bought from Nets in 2019, FT reports
item.description: Mastercard is seeking to sell the real-time payments unit that it acquired from Denmark's вҖӢNets Group in 2019 for $3.2 billion, the Financial вҖҢTimes reported on Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Apple plans to open Siri to rival AI services, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: Apple plans to open its Siri voice assistant to rival artificial intelligence services beyond its current вҖӢpartnership with ChatGPT, Bloomberg News reported on вҖҢThursday, citing people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Exclusive: Musk rewrites IPO playbook with large slice of SpaceX stock for retail investors, source says
item.description: Elon Musk is discussing allocating as much as 30% of SpaceXвҖҷs initial public offering to individual investors вҖ” at least three вҖӢtimes the usual retail slice вҖ” leaning on his rabid fan base and other loyal backers to help steady the stock after its debut, a person вҖҢfamiliar with the matter said.

item.title: Trump officials tout US energy dominance as global oil execs warn of supply crisis
item.description: U.S. officials this week said the historic spike in fuel prices during the war in Iran will be short-term and trumpeted record U.S. production at an industry conference where oil executives and government officials вҖӢfrom Asia, the Middle East and Europe bemoaned the worst oil and gas supply disruption in decades.

item.title: CERAWEEK Private firms spearhead global shale push as US public operators stayВ home
item.description: Private energy companies and their backers are emerging as leaders вҖӢin the next phase of global shale development, taking early positions in overseas basins while publicly listed U.S. producers focus on capital discipline and their core вҖҢdomestic acreage.

item.title: Microsoft freezes hiring in major cloud, sales groups, The Information reports
item.description: Microsoft executives вҖӢhave in recent вҖҢweeks told managers вҖӢat вҖӢmajor divisions, including вҒ its вҖӢcloud unit вҖӢand North American sales groups, вҖӢto вҖӢsuspend new hiring, вҖҢThe вҒ Information reported on Thursday, citing вҖӢthree вҖӢemployees вҒ with direct вҖӢknowledge of вҖӢthe вҒ decision.

item.title: Musk urges judge to review Twitter verdict, accuses jury of 'mocking' him
item.description: A lawyer вҖӢfor Elon Musk urged a federal judge to review a recent jury verdict finding that Musk вҖҢdefrauded Twitter investors when buying the social media company, saying jurors improperly used the verdict to "send a message" by finding him liable.

item.title: Bank of Canada says it will have a tough job tackling structural changes
item.description: By Promit Mukherjee and David Ljunggren

item.title: United Airlines reaches tentative labor deal with flight attendants
item.description: United вҖӢAirlines flight вҖҢattendants have reached вҖӢa вҖӢnew tentative labor вҒ agreement вҖӢwith the вҖӢcompany, their union said вҖӢon вҖӢThursday.

item.title: Crypto platform Anchorage brings Sun's Tron to US investors
item.description: Anchorage Digital, a federally regulated U.S. crypto platform, said on Thursday it would add Justin Sun's Tron вҖӢblockchain to its network, expanding the venture's access to U.S. вҖҢinvestors.

item.title: Stryker says manufacturing mostly restored after cyberattack
item.description: Medical device maker Stryker said on Thursday its operations were steadily improving towards full вҖӢcapacity, after a cyberattack caused widespread вҖҢdisruption to its business.

item.title: Pernod Ricard has held merger talks with Jack Daniel's maker, source says
item.description: France's Pernod Ricard and Jack Daniel's owner BrownвҖ‘Forman have held talks about a possible merger, a source familiar with the вҖӢmatter told Reuters, a move that would unite the world's second-largest spirits maker with the largest вҖҢplayer in American whiskey.

item.title: Meta shares slip after US jury verdicts raise concerns of new legal exposure
item.description: Meta shares fell 6% to a 10-month low on Thursday after rulings this week found the Facebook parent failed to adequately warn or вҖӢprotect young users, stoking concerns about billions of dollars in fines вҖҢfrom new cases and follow-on litigation.

item.title: Peso mexicano se negocia estable atento a Medio Oriente, aguarda decisiГіn Banxico
item.description: El peso mexicano mostraba pocos cambios el jueves, mientras que la bolsa caГӯa en un mercado sin rumbo debido a la cautela de los вҖӢinversionistas atentos a noticias sobre la posibilidad de un pronto fin del conflicto вҖҢen Medio Oriente.

item.title: Dutch court orders xAI, Grok not to create, distribute nonconsensual sex images in Netherlands
item.description: A Dutch вҖҢcourt вҖӢon вҖӢThursday ordered вҒ Elon вҖӢMusk's xAI вҖӢand chatbot вҖӢGrok вҖӢnot to generate вҖҢand вҒ distribute sexualized вҖӢimages вҖӢof вҒ people вҖӢwithout вҖӢtheir вҒ consent in вҒ the вҖӢNetherlands.

item.title: US fixed 30-year mortgage rate jumps to six-month high as Iran war drags on
item.description: The average rate on the popular U.S. 30-year fixed-rate mortgage has вҖӢjumped to a six-month high as вҖҢrising oil prices from the dragging Middle East war fanned inflation worries, which could вҖӢweigh on home sales during the вҖӢtypically busy spring season.

item.title: Judge dismisses lawsuit by Musk's X Corp accusing advertisers of illegal boycott
item.description: A U.S. judge вҖӢon Thursday dismissed вҖҢX Corp's antitrust lawsuit that accused вҖӢthe World вҖӢFederation of Advertisers and вҒ a group вҖӢof major companies вҖӢincluding Mars, CVS Health and Colgate-Palmolive of illegally вҖӢboycotting billionaire вҖӢentrepreneur Elon MuskвҖҷs social вҖҢmedia вҒ company.

item.title: Novo Nordisk appoints Mars CEO as board observer
item.description: Novo Nordisk on Thursday elected Poul Weihrauch, CEO вҖӢof family-owned U.S. candy and pet food вҖҢgiant Mars, as observer to its board, as the drugmaker seeks to strengthen its position in the highly вҖӢcompetitive U.S. obesity market.

item.title: Tom Brady-backed telehealth firm eMed valued at over $2 billion in latest funding round
item.description: Telehealth firm eMed said on Thursday it has raised $200 million in its latest funding round, вҖӢvaluing the Miami, Florida-based company at more than $2 billion.

item.title: Blackstone invests $250 million in UAE despite war disruption
item.description: Blackstone has invested $250 million in Advanced Digital Gaming Technology, a new payments and data intelligence technology platform based in the вҖӢUnited Arab Emirates, the asset manager said in a statement вҖҢon Thursday.

item.title: GlobalFoundries files patent infringement lawsuits against Tower Semiconductor
item.description: GlobalFoundries on вҖӢThursday said it has sued Israel-based rival вҖҢTower Semiconductor , alleging that Tower Semiconductor infringed on 11 GlobalFoundries patents that relate to manufacturing chips that go вҖӢinto smart phones and other electronic вҖӢdevices.
